items.title: "The Hawks didn't go BPA! Onsi lied!"
items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
"The Hawks didn't go BPA! Onsi lied!"

I've seen variations of this sentiment all night and morning, and I don't really get why?

Zuby wasn't my personal BPA either, but it was very obviously a BPA pick on THEIR board.

It's not as if they view combo big as a position of need.

items.title: An incredible note from Jim's wide-ranging story:
items.author: Dan Devine
items.body_excerpt: An incredible note from Jim's wide-ranging story:

images.alt:
Beyond a pair of double doors inside the lower-level bowl of Fiserv Forum, a hallway leads to the Bucks locker room. Following teh signing of his maximum contract extension in the winter of 2020, it was adorned with Antetokounmpo's Nike logo, an interlocking "GA."

At the start of the 2024-25 season, Rivers' first full campaign as head coach, the "GA" was removed. Instead, the team emblazoned the word "together" in different languages.

The English version of the word was misspelled.

items.title: Hollander POTD Acie Earl. Iowa PF/C was drafted 19th by BOS in 93. Struggled to get time in BOS and was taken by expansion TOR, where he put up 7.6 ppg in 95-96 and had a 40-point game. After 97, left NBA for Europe/Australia/Asia thru 04. Has coached since retiring.

items.title: For seven years, AJ Dybantsa dreamed about being the first pick in the NBA Draft. And when it actually happened last night, the reality was better than his dream.
items.author: Josh Robbins
items.body_excerpt:
For seven years, AJ Dybantsa dreamed about being the first pick in the NBA Draft. And when it actually happened last night, the reality was better than his dream. 

My interview with the newest Washington Wizards for @TheAthleticNBA, free to read:
www.nytimes.com/athletic/738...

media.title: A conversation with AJ Dybantsa: ‘We can do some big things in the future’ (Gift Article)
media.description: “I’ve been dreaming about this since I was about 12 years old," Dybantsa said.

items.title: One thing I was wrong about last night is thinking that at least one of the first round guards would still be available at 23.
items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
One thing I was wrong about last night is thinking that at least one of the first round guards would still be available at 23.

Philon ended up as the last one at 22.

So the theoretical/popular “draft Mara and then take a guard at 23” idea probably wouldn’t have been an option.

items.title: It’s always hysterical to me that post-draft reaction is often framed as if it is the final part of the NBA offseason when it’s actually the first part.
items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
It’s always hysterical to me that post-draft reaction is often framed as if it is the final part of the NBA offseason when it’s actually the first part.

(Obviously, the Hawks and the center position is a clear example of this as if they aren’t allowed to add anyone else now)

The Spurs drafted David Robinson, and went 56-26.
